justification: O paper descreve um sistema multi-stage para deployment em edge-cloud: Abstract Workflow Designer → Constraints Manager → Service Catalog → Candidate Discovery → LLM Advising. A Seção IV.E (LLM-Based Deployment Advising) revela que o LLM funciona como componente de advisory APÓS a descoberta de candidatos, gerando recomendações via prompt estruturado ('You are a multi-cloud expert... choose the best cloud service'). O LLM não participa de um loop autônomo de controle (perceive-reason-act) que DIRIJA decisões de placement; ele apenas aconselha. Este é o padrão 'LLM supports other method' explicitamente excluído pela boundary.

evidence.architectural_pattern: Multi-stage pipeline with LLM as advisory post-processing component, not agentic loop.
evidence.infrastructure_coupling: Framework operates externally to cloud/edge orchestrators; LLM provides deployment advice, not autonomous control.
evidence.rm_decision_class: LLM role is recommendation/advising, not autonomous placement decision-making.
evidence.reasoning_process: Structured prompt-based reasoning to generate deployment insights; no closed-loop perception-action.
evidence.grounding: Service catalog, constraints metadata; no continuous telemetry-driven feedback.
evidence.evaluation: Case study validation; no formal RM metrics (latency, utilization, SLA).
evidence.key_quote: IV.E: 'leverage reasoning capabilities of LLMs to determine optimal deployment configuration... LLMs are guided by structured prompt… to produce practical deployment insights.'

notes: LLM role is decision-support (advising) in a multi-stage framework, not agentic driver of RM loop. The pipeline separates service discovery (Candidate Discovery) from LLM recommendation (LLM Advising), indicating LLM does not autonomously control placement. Weighted score 0.47 < threshold 0.55. Alignment with regex category 'H_llm_supports_other_method' confirms exclusion.
